AnmolSaini16/mapcn ·GitHub ⭐ 6354 TypeScript · GIS, React Components, MapLibre GL, Tailwind CSS, Frontend Development, Open Source

What it is

mapcn is a collection of React components for integrating powerful maps into web applications. Built on MapLibre GL and styled with Tailwind CSS, it follows the design patterns of shadcn/ui. The components are theme-aware, composable, and come with sensible defaults for markers, popups, and routes, requiring 'zero config' for basic setups.

Problems solved

  • Complex map integration: Simplifies MapLibre GL setup into declarative React components.
  • Design inconsistency: Adapts automatically to light/dark themes and matches shadcn/ui aesthetics.
  • Boilerplate code: Provides one-command setup for common features like markers, zoom controls, and fullscreen modes.
  • Basemap licensing confusion: Clearly defines terms for CARTO Basemaps and provides alternatives.

How to get started

Install via npx or your preferred package manager. Import components like MapContainer, Marker, or Route into your React project. The maps work out of the box with default styles, but can be fully customized using Tailwind or MapLibre's underlying API.

5. Code Organization

The repository is structured logically, with core functionality separated from utilities and examples. This organization facilitates easy navigation and contribution for developers looking to extend the tool's capabilities.

6. Quick Start Suggestion

To get started, install via npx or your preferred package manager. import components like mapcontainer, marker, or route into your react project. the maps work out of the box with default styles, but can be fully customized using tailwind or maplibre's underlying api. This will allow you to quickly evaluate the tool and integrate it into your existing development workflow.

7. Summary

An essential, TypeScript-powered utility that streamlines beautiful map components. 100% free, zero config, one command setup; it effectively tackles Complex map integration: Simplifies MapLibre GL setup into declarative React components. while maintaining a high standard of code quality and usability.
